title: You Kent Always Say What You Want

jokes.text: Make room for Jumbo. - What'd you say? - Nothing.
jokes.setup: Family gathering for portrait, typical tension building
jokes.punchline: Bart calls Homer 'Jumbo' and immediately denies it when confronted
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart's disrespectful behavior perfectly integrated with his character, economical delivery with instant backpedal

jokes.text: Praise the Lord. - Watch your mouth, you little smart-ass. - Yeah, Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart says something seemingly innocent
jokes.punchline: Homer scolds him for the innocent phrase while using actual profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic reversal - Homer condemns innocent religious expression while cursing. Perfect character hypocrisy.

jokes.text: Why, you little... [Homer presumably strangles Bart as photo is taken]
jokes.setup: Elaborate setup of family trying to take perfect portrait
jokes.punchline: Homer starts strangling Bart right as the photo is taken, ruining it
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons physical comedy - the entire portrait setup pays off with Homer's signature move at worst possible moment

jokes.text: American Applesauce Association?
jokes.setup: Car won't start, Marge mentions using Homer's AAA card
jokes.punchline: AAA stands for something completely ridiculous rather than Auto Association
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of expected acronym. Economical and absurd in perfect Simpsons fashion.

jokes.text: We're going to have to run. There isn't even time to stretch my quads. My quads! Every stride is a nightmare!
jokes.setup: Marge realizes they need to run to appointment
jokes.punchline: Immediately regrets not stretching, then suffers dramatically while running
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from concern to immediate suffering. Shows Marge's middle-aged reality.

jokes.text: Dentist? You said we were going to ride dirt bikes around the cemetery.
jokes.setup: Kids arrive home, Marge mentions dentist appointment
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals the ridiculous lie Marge told to get him there
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and absurd lie that perfectly captures desperate parenting and Bart's gullibility

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, you fall for that every six months.
jokes.setup: Bart's surprise at the dentist appointment
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals this is a recurring pattern of deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that makes the absurd lie even funnier by revealing it's systematic deception

jokes.text: Why the cemetery, I wondered, but my dreams were too strong.
jokes.setup: Homer overhears the cemetery dirt bike plan
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal monologue reveals he questioned it but was too excited to care
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's childlike enthusiasm overriding logic. 'Dreams were too strong' is beautifully absurd phrasing

jokes.text: Absolutely. All the time. I go to the floss store and they're like, 'Whoa, there he is again.' 'Cause I buy so much floss.
jokes.setup: Dentist asks if Homer flosses regularly
jokes.punchline: Homer's elaborate lie about being a floss store celebrity
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature over-elaborate lying. The 'floss store' concept and celebrity status there is hilariously specific

jokes.text: I know, but a breeze from the window went in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Homer screams about gum pain, dentist says he hasn't started yet
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic explanation for his pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cowardice - finds the most ridiculous excuse for his dental sensitivity

jokes.text: I don't even have a special tooth problem! This is just a routine checkup!
jokes.setup: Marge tries to comfort Bart about the screaming man
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals his screaming is just for a routine visit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's pathetic dental fear, makes his cowardice even more embarrassing

jokes.text: Oh, the bib is choking me!
jokes.setup: Homer continues being dramatic at dentist
jokes.punchline: Even the paper bib is torturous to him
jokes.explanation: Continues Homer's dental cowardice theme, though less inventive than previous examples

jokes.text: Damn, baby got bacteria.
jokes.setup: Educational dental video begins
jokes.punchline: Parodies 'Baby Got Back' with dental terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture parody adapted for dental health. Good economy and memorable

jokes.text: Luda-Crest.
jokes.setup: Ludacris-style rapper in dental video
jokes.punchline: Name combines Ludacris with Crest toothpaste
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective portmanteau. Maximum economy and clear concept

jokes.text: This film is against tooth decay, but it also kind of glamorizes it.
jokes.setup: Watching the hip-hop dental video
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out the contradictory message of making decay seem cool
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa observation about mixed messages in media. Smart meta-commentary on the preceding sequence

jokes.text: This is the greatest movie ever. - Dad, that's Ludicrous! - I have a right to my views. - No! I mean Chris 'Ludacris' Bridges,
jokes.setup: Homer loves the dental video, Lisa calls it ludicrous
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks she's attacking his opinion, but she means the actual rapper Ludacris
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay misdirection that plays on Homer's defensive nature and the rapper's name

jokes.text: This is the greatest movie ever. / Dad, that's Ludicrous! / I have a right to my views. / No! I mean Chris 'Ludacris' Bridges, right here in the dental plaza!
jokes.setup: Homer calling the dental video the greatest movie ever, Lisa saying 'that's ludicrous'
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks she's insulting his opinion, but she means the actual rapper Ludacris is there
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection comedy with wordplay, Homer's defensiveness makes it work

jokes.text: Expect a letter from Cap In The Ass Productions and its head legal counsel, Ms. Melissa Burlingame!
jokes.setup: Ludacris threatens legal action for unauthorized showing
jokes.punchline: Production company name is crude pun, but lawyer has completely normal name
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between ridiculous company name and mundane lawyer name. Slightly cheap on the profanity

jokes.text: So you're saying I should do your job for you, at home, for free. You wish.
jokes.setup: Dentist gives free toothbrush, says keep teeth clean
jokes.punchline: Homer sees through the economics of dental hygiene
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly sharp economic observation from Homer. Good subversion of his usual stupidity

jokes.text: Good afternoon, Principal Stinkface. - Dr. Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart role-playing as dentist with drugged Skinner
jokes.punchline: Skinner accepts the insulting name and plays along
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal setup with drugged authority figure. Skinner's compliance makes it work

jokes.text: Uh-oh, I'm feeling a lot of cooties down there. We may need to replace a tooth.
jokes.setup: Bart examining drugged Skinner
jokes.punchline: Bart uses childish 'cooties' terminology for dental problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - mixing childish vocabulary with adult authority. Good character integration

jokes.text: What the hell is a fundae? - It's a sundae that's fun.
jokes.setup: Homer reads menu at ice cream shop
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'fun' and 'sundae' explained with circular logic
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke. Functional but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Snickles, Gooey Bears, Charlottesville Chew, Nice 'N' Many, Kat Kit, Herschel's Smooches, Mrs. Bad Bar, and Mi Dudes.
jokes.setup: Homer ordering 'healthy' ice cream with mix-ins
jokes.punchline: List of parody candy names that sound similar to real brands
jokes.explanation: Creative parody names. Good Simpsons trademark of almost-but-not-quite real brand names

jokes.text: Of course that has nothing to do with the fact that this station and the ice-cream company are owned by the same corporation, but I digress.
jokes.setup: Kent introduces story about Homer's ice cream purchase
jokes.punchline: Kent directly acknowledges corporate synergy conflict of interest
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire. Kent breaking fourth wall about corporate influence is both funny and pointed

jokes.text: I've never been to war, but I imagine it feels just like your first kill.
jokes.setup: Asked how it felt to buy the ice cream cone
jokes.punchline: Homer compares ice cream purchase to murder in war
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and absurd comparison. Perfect Homer logic leap from mundane to horrific. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It won't come off. - Where's the hammer?
jokes.setup: Etch-A-Sketch won't clear the drawn swear word
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution is to destroy it with a hammer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer escalation - immediately jumps to destruction. Good payoff to Etch-A-Sketch gag

jokes.text: I said a word so vile it should only be uttered by Satan himself while sitting on the toilet.
jokes.setup: Kent apologizing for his on-air profanity
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and grotesque description of how bad the word was
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific escalation. The toilet detail makes it absurdly vivid and memorable

jokes.text: Oh, God, no, I get my news from the Internet like every other normal person under 70. Farewell, dinosaur.
jokes.setup: Kent asks stranger if he saw his broadcast
jokes.punchline: Dismisses traditional TV news as obsolete, calls Kent a dinosaur
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary on changing media landscape. 'Farewell, dinosaur' is a nice cutting dismissal

jokes.text: 'booby,' 'tushie,' 'burp,' 'fanny burp,' 'water closet,' 'underpants,' 'dingle-dangle,' 'Boston marriage,' 'LBJ,' 'Titica,' 'hot dog,' or 'front lumps'!
jokes.setup: Grampa complaining about modern TV standards
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous list of allegedly forbidden words from innocent to bizarre
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa rambling with increasingly absurd 'forbidden' words. Mix of innocent and mysterious terms

jokes.text: You mean there are losers who spend all day watching TV looking for stuff to complain about? - Who'd be lame enough to do that?
jokes.setup: Discussion about religious watchdog groups monitoring TV
jokes.punchline: Cut to Ned doing exactly that activity
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for visual reveal. Good structure but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Smallville... super dog licks himself. That definitely goes in the naughty pile.
jokes.setup: Ned monitoring TV for offensive content
jokes.punchline: Natural dog behavior is considered scandalous
jokes.explanation: Good Ned prudishness. The innocent dog behavior being 'naughty' shows his extreme standards

jokes.text: 60 Minutes... Morley Safer has questionable pants fold.
jokes.setup: Continuing Ned's TV monitoring
jokes.punchline: Even clothing wrinkles are morally suspicious to Ned
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Ned's prudishness. 'Questionable pants fold' is hilariously specific

jokes.text: The L Word... Used the 'L' word.
jokes.setup: Ned continuing his moral TV monitoring
jokes.punchline: Show called 'The L Word' is offensive for using the L word
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the show's title. Perfect deadpan delivery from character's perspective

jokes.text: Imploring people I never met to pressure a government with better things to do to punish a man who meant no harm for something nobody even saw. That's what I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Ned's daughter asks what he's doing
jokes.punchline: Ned perfectly describes the absurdity of his own crusade
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware description that exposes the absurdity of moral panic while staying in character

jokes.text: Daddy, we think you need a new mommy. - First things first.
jokes.setup: Kids suggest Ned needs a new wife
jokes.punchline: Ned prioritizes his crusade over finding love
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Ned's priorities and widowerhood. Kids' practical concern vs. his obsession

jokes.text: Even the map thinks you stink, Kent.
jokes.setup: Kent doing weather, map malfunctions
jokes.punchline: Arnie suggests the technical failure is personal judgment
jokes.explanation: Good Arnie Pye antagonism. Anthropomorphizing technical equipment for insult is clever

jokes.text: Looks like Newshound, the Channel Six mascot, is gonna have to go without his lazy-eye surgery. - Sorry, boy. - No, I'm over here. Over here!
jokes.setup: News about budget cuts affecting mascot's medical needs
jokes.punchline: Mascot's lazy eye prevents him from getting attention, making problem worse
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about medical condition creating self-perpetuating problem. Good visual gag

jokes.text: Looks like Newshound, the Channel Six mascot, is gonna have to go without his lazy-eye surgery. Sorry, boy. No, I'm over here. Over here! I got a treat for you. Oh! Now he's gonna starve.
jokes.setup: Station cutting costs due to FCC fine affects mascot's medical care
jokes.punchline: Mascot's lazy eye means he can't see treats being offered, creating more problems
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy escalation. Medical condition creates immediate practical problems in darkly funny way.

jokes.text: I'm a cat walking down the street, swinging my arms. Look, a lady made of dynamite! Wait, I don't know that yet.
jokes.setup: Krusty doing voices for Itchy and Scratchy due to budget cuts
jokes.punchline: Krusty breaks character to note he shouldn't know information yet
jokes.explanation: Good meta-comedy about narrative perspective. Krusty's amateur voice acting creates fourth-wall breaks

jokes.text: Damn that Brockman! There are only two rules in TV: don't swear, and don't whip it out. It's not rocket science.
jokes.setup: Executive upset about Kent's scandal
jokes.punchline: Reduces all TV standards to two basic rules, second being extreme
jokes.explanation: Excellent distillation of broadcast standards to absurd minimums. Second rule escalates perfectly

jokes.text: Say, what's that powder in your coffee? Is it cocaine? - No, it's Splenda. - Spare me your slang, pusher man. You're fired!
jokes.setup: Executive questions Kent about powder in coffee
jokes.punchline: Refuses to believe innocent explanation, treats artificial sweetener as drug slang
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection where innocent explanation sounds more suspicious. 'Pusher man' is perfect paranoid escalation

jokes.text: That's because you're sitting on me, you lummox! - Kent Brockman!
jokes.setup: Homer complains couch is lumpy
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals he's sitting on their houseguest
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup. Functional but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: Did you know that every day Mexican gays sneak into this country and unplug our brain-dead ladies?
jokes.setup: Marge mentions Homer watches Fox News
jokes.punchline: Homer recites completely nonsensical conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of conspiracy theories and media manipulation. Absurd enough to be clearly satirical

jokes.text: Dad, you barely go to work at all. - And you're constantly flouting the law. - I'm willing to change my mind and that makes me great.
jokes.setup: Homer claims to work hard and play by rules, kids contradict him
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately changes position and claims that makes him great
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - turning obvious character flaws into virtues. Great self-delusion

jokes.text: Apu sang a song. What are you gonna do?
jokes.setup: Homer pointing to wall of casual acquaintances
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of presumably elaborate musical performance
jokes.explanation: Good callback humor. Homer's dismissive attitude toward what was likely elaborate performance

jokes.text: Fox deliberately runs shows that will earn them huge fines which are then funneled through the FCC straight to the Republican party.
jokes.setup: Kent explaining media conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Accuses Fox of elaborate scheme involving FCC fines and political funding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Fox network airing The Simpsons. Sharp political satire with insider knowledge

jokes.text: they could spoon on a twin mattress and still have room for Ted Koppel.
jokes.setup: Kent describes intimate relationship between press and government
jokes.punchline: Specific image of how close they are, with random journalist reference
jokes.explanation: Vivid and specific metaphor for political corruption. Ted Koppel reference adds absurdist element

jokes.text: Now, like toothless babies, they suckle on the sugary teat of misinformation and poop it into the diaper we call the 6:00 news.
jokes.setup: Kent's continued rant about journalism
jokes.punchline: Extended baby metaphor for how news media processes information
jokes.explanation: Disgusting but effective extended metaphor. 'Sugary teat of misinformation' is particularly memorable

jokes.text: I could Dracula bite him.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how to stop Kent
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony offers vampire attack as assassination method
jokes.explanation: Hilariously innocent way to describe murder. Fat Tony's childlike approach to violence

jokes.text: Every order is half this, half that, it's confusing!
jokes.setup: Krusty criticizes Fat Tony's pizza order mistakes
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony defends himself with reasonable complaint about complex orders
jokes.explanation: Good humanization of Fat Tony. Makes mob boss relatable through mundane pizza problems

jokes.text: For years now, Fox has been... programming shows that the whole family can enjoy.
jokes.setup: Homer building up to reveal terrible Fox secret
jokes.punchline: The 'scandal' is completely wholesome programming
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts conspiracy expectations. Meta-commentary on Fox's actual programming strategy
